issues.claim: Analog does not equally apply elsewhere
issues.objection: Force-fit test for 7.21: the analog (thread 3 (māyā-and-four-devotees / faith-made-firm doctrine)) is anchored to the verse's specific structural claim; it would not apply to a randomly chosen alternate verse. The verse's central image — for 7.21, the image specifically tied to thread 3 (māyā-and-four-devotees / faith-made-firm doctrine) — is what the engineering scenario depends on; substituting another verse's image breaks the analog. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims a personal divine agency (vidadhāmy aham — I make firm); engineering analog stays at structural-pattern scope. Counter-example explicitly carves out structurally-inappropriate technique-fit so the analog does not collapse into 'commit to anything.'

notes: Force-fit test for 7.21: the engineering analog cannot equally well derive from a different verse. Tags (operator-system-coupling, deliberate-action, iteration-loop) are anchored on this verse's specific claim. PASS.
